findings.title: Error responses missing code field (clients can't match on stable identifier)
findings.evidence: reply.status(404).send({ error: 'Order not found' }); -- no code: ErrorCodes.ORDER_NOT_FOUND. Other call sites in the same module use ErrorCodes correctly. Affects admin GET /admin/orders/:id.
findings.fix_sketch: Import ErrorCodes and add code: ErrorCodes.ORDER_NOT_FOUND to the response payload. Apply same pattern to all error sends in this module.

findings.title: Magic string error code REVIEW_NOT_OWNED not declared in ErrorCodes
findings.evidence: code: 'REVIEW_NOT_OWNED' is a string literal that does NOT exist in apps/backend/src/errors/codes.ts. Clients matching on code will receive a code that is not part of the published contract. Centralized error handler in index.ts also won't map it to a status code, so the response uses the inline 403 (works by accident).
findings.fix_sketch: Add REVIEW_NOT_OWNED: 'REVIEW_NOT_OWNED' to ErrorCodes in apps/backend/src/errors/codes.ts, then import and use ErrorCodes.REVIEW_NOT_OWNED. Also add a 403 mapping in the index.ts codeToStatus table.

findings.title: upload.route.merchant.ts performs plan-limit check inside handler body, not as preHandler
findings.evidence: Handler body contains: try { await planLimitsService.checkStorageLimit(...) } catch (err) { if (code === ErrorCodes.PLAN_LIMIT_EXCEEDED) reply.status(403).send(...); throw err; }. The same anti-pattern is already used (and accepted) in product.route.merchant.ts:42 and staff.route.merchant.ts:29. Hook belongs in apps/backend/src/scopes/ as e.g. requireStorageQuota().
findings.fix_sketch: Move the checkStorageLimit wrapping logic into a requireStorageQuota() hook in apps/backend/src/scopes/, then chain it in the route's preHandler array.

findings.title: Webhook signature/raw-body validation errors lack code field
findings.evidence: Four 400 responses: reply.status(400).send({ error: 'Bad Request', message: 'Missing webhook signature' }) and 'Missing raw request body' -- all without code: ErrorCodes.VALIDATION_ERROR. The same file uses codes for 422/404 elsewhere.
findings.fix_sketch: Add code: ErrorCodes.VALIDATION_ERROR to each of the four webhook signature/raw-body error responses.

findings.title: Transient payment-webhook 500 response lacks code field
findings.evidence: reply.status(500).send({ error: 'Internal Server Error', message: 'Transient error, will retry' }) -- no code. Provider (Razorpay/Stripe) retries on 5xx but the client log won't have a stable error code to match.
findings.fix_sketch: Add code: 'INTERNAL_ERROR' (or add PAYMENT_TRANSIENT_ERROR to ErrorCodes) for stable identification. If the intent is to deliberately hide details, keep the message generic but add a stable code.

findings.title: Health-check 503/403 responses lack code field
findings.evidence: Eight health-check responses use { error: 'DB pool saturated' }, 'Service unavailable', or 'Forbidden' with no code. Monitoring clients can't distinguish DB saturation from generic unavailability.
findings.fix_sketch: Add ErrorCodes.SERVICE_UNAVAILABLE / HEALTH_CHECK_UNAUTHORIZED (or reuse NOT_FOUND) to codes.ts and set code on each health-check response.

findings.title: Swagger plugin auth/error responses lack code field
findings.evidence: Four responses from the Swagger basic-auth plugin: { error: 'Not Found' }, 'Unauthorized', 'Server Error'. No code: ErrorCodes.X mapping.
findings.fix_sketch: Add ErrorCodes.SWAGGER_AUTH_REQUIRED / SWAGGER_CONFIG_ERROR (or reuse existing INSUFFICIENT_PERMISSIONS / NOT_FOUND) and set code on each response.

findings.title: Product CSV import 400 responses lack code field
findings.evidence: Three 400 responses: 'No file uploaded', 'CSV is empty or missing header', 'Missing required columns' -- no code: ErrorCodes.VALIDATION_ERROR. The file already uses ErrorCodes in other branches.
findings.fix_sketch: Add code: ErrorCodes.VALIDATION_ERROR to each of the three CSV import 400 responses.

findings.title: Bulk ': any' usage in dashboard Svelte routes (63 instances across 38 files)
findings.evidence: grep ': any' yields 63 matches across 38 dashboard files. Common patterns: catch (err: any) instead of unknown, callback parameters (openEdit(product: any), openEdit(coupon: any), .find((p: any) => ...), .map((r: any) => ...)). Erodes the Svelte 5 type safety the project invested in.
findings.fix_sketch: Define DTO interfaces (Product, Coupon, Plan, Review, etc.) and use them. Replace catch (err: any) with catch (err: unknown) { const msg = err instanceof Error ? err.message : String(err); }. Type callback parameters explicitly.

findings.title: ': any' usage in storefront-food Svelte routes (12 instances across 6 files)
findings.evidence: 12 matches across 6 files (cart, checkout, menu, +page, +layout). Notable: stripe: any, cardElement: any in checkout/+page.svelte:46-47, .find((c: any) => ...) in addToCart, catch (e: any) in initStripe. Includes a @ts-expect-error suppression for window.Stripe.
findings.fix_sketch: Define MenuItem / CartItem / OrderItem interfaces in src/lib/types/. For Stripe, import @stripe/stripe-js types instead of suppressing with @ts-expect-error. Replace catch (e: any) with unknown.

findings.title: Kebab-case module folders break naming convention
findings.evidence: All other 35 module folders use camelCase (abandonedCart, planLimits). The two outliers use kebab-case (abandoned-cart, plan-limits) which forces import paths like './abandoned-cart.service.js' and prevents the usual camelCase identifier convention.
findings.fix_sketch: Rename folders to abandonedCart and planLimits. Update all import sites (cart.service.ts:18, jobs/abandonedCartCron.ts:38, etc.).

findings.title: staff.constants.ts is the only .constants.ts file in modules
findings.evidence: Naming convention: services = *.service.ts, repos = *.repo.ts, routes = *.route*.ts, schemas = *.schema.ts, types = *.types.ts. DEFAULT_ROLE_PERMISSIONS is a constant but not exported from a service. Either move into staff.service.ts or rename file to staff.permissions.ts to follow established suffix pattern.
findings.fix_sketch: Move DEFAULT_ROLE_PERMISSIONS export into staff.service.ts (or staff.repo.ts) as a top-level const so the file matches the project naming.

findings.title: Centralized error handler uses string-literal codes (decoupled from ErrorCodes)
findings.evidence: The 60+ entry codeToStatus map uses string literals (NOT_FOUND, INVALID_CREDENTIALS, PLAN_EXPIRED, etc.) instead of ErrorCodes.NOT_FOUND etc. If a code in errors/codes.ts is renamed, the status mapping silently breaks (and TypeScript won't catch it).
findings.fix_sketch: Import ErrorCodes and use the constant values: VALIDATION_ERROR: 400 becomes [ErrorCodes.VALIDATION_ERROR]: 400. Or generate the map programmatically with a helper.

findings.title: Landing +page.svelte is 622 lines (above 500-line threshold)
findings.evidence: Single marketing landing-page component at 622 lines. Contains hero, features, testimonials, pricing, FAQ, footer in one file.
findings.fix_sketch: Split into Hero, Features, Testimonials, Pricing, FAQ, Footer subcomponents in apps/landing/src/lib/components/. Keep +page.svelte as composition only.

findings.title: payment.service.ts is 672 lines (above 500-line threshold)
findings.evidence: Single service file at 672 lines handles Stripe, Razorpay, manual/COD, webhooks, refunds, and provider discovery. Single biggest service in the repo.
findings.fix_sketch: Split into payment.service.ts (orchestration), paymentStripe.service.ts, paymentRazorpay.service.ts, paymentWebhook.service.ts, and keep a thin facade for backward compat.

findings.title: auth.route.customer.ts is 510 lines (above 500-line threshold)
findings.evidence: Single route file at 510 lines covers register, login, logout, refresh, profile, password reset, email verify, MFA enable, MFA verify, invite-accept. Same pattern in merchant route.
findings.fix_sketch: Split into auth.route.customer.register.ts, auth.route.customer.password.ts, auth.route.customer.mfa.ts, auth.route.customer.profile.ts, etc.

findings.title: seo.route.public.ts returns 404 as bare object (no status from throw, no code)
findings.evidence: GET /products/:id/jsonld does: if (!jsonLd) { reply.status(404); return { error: 'Product not found' }; }. Missing code: ErrorCodes.PRODUCT_NOT_FOUND. Status is set but the response shape diverges from the rest of the API.
findings.fix_sketch: Use reply.status(404).send({ error: 'Not Found', code: ErrorCodes.PRODUCT_NOT_FOUND, message: 'Product not found' }) for consistency with the rest of the codebase.

findings.title: console.log in CLI scripts (migrate.ts, seed-superadmin.ts, db/seed.ts, db/check-schema.ts)
findings.evidence: 58 console.log calls, all in CLI scripts (migrate, seed, check-schema) which are run once via tsx, not imported by the Fastify server. Each file already has an eslint-disable no-console comment. Acceptable for one-shot CLI tools.
findings.fix_sketch: No action -- these are intentional CLI scripts that don't have access to fastify.log. Document in CLAUDE.md that console.log is allowed in scripts/migrate/seed files only.
